definition: The U.S. is transitioning from a comfortable r<g regime (2010-2021) to r≈g (2023-present). Effective interest rate on federal debt has risen from ~1.5% (2021) to ~3.3% (2024), converging toward marginal rates (~4.5-5.0%). Cross-country base rate: when r≈g (within ±100bp), 35-40% probability of fiscal stress within 10 years.
evidence: CBO mechanical projections of interest cost trajectory
Blanchard 2019: r<g necessary for sustainability without primary surpluses
IMF panel (Mauro & Zhou 2021): bimodal outcomes when r≈g
Base rate of advanced economies achieving 3-4pp primary adjustment without recession: ~30-40%
Interest payments projected to reach ~4% GDP by 2030
open_questions: Does reserve currency status change the sustainability arithmetic?
Can 'beautiful deleveraging' (moderate inflation + growth + mild repression over decades) work?
What is the market-implied probability of fiscal stress?
